apply: Use the binomial nomenclature to identify and classify organisms
Apply the principles of cladistics to infer evolutionary relationships among organisms
Use the characteristics of different taxonomic ranks to classify an unknown organism
analyze: Compare and contrast different taxonomic classification systems
Analyze the limitations and advantages of different taxonomic systems
Evaluate the impact of biological classification on our understanding of evolutionary relationships
remember: Explain the concept of biological classification
List the major characteristics of different taxonomic ranks
Recall the binomial nomenclature and the use of Latin names in taxonomy
understand: Describe the hierarchy of taxonomic ranks and their relationships
Explain the concept of cladistics and its application in biological classification
Discuss the significance of biological classification in understanding evolutionary relationships

ncert_objectives: Explain the importance of biological classification and its relevance to the study of biology.
Outline the major characteristics of the different taxonomic ranks, i.e., Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus and Species.
Describe the binomial nomenclature and the use of Latin names in taxonomy.
board_exam_objectives: What are the major characteristics of different taxonomic ranks?
Describe the binomial nomenclature and its application in taxonomy.
Explain the importance of biological classification in understanding evolutionary relationships among organisms.
